clusters.title: Apple names new chief executive to replace Tim Cook
clusters.neutral_headline: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
bullet_summary: I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release
1, the company said in a statement Monday
Bloomberg News previously reported that Ternus was Cook’s heir apparent

items.title: Apple names new chief executive to replace Tim Cook
items.standfirst: John Ternus will take over running the technology giant as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.
items.content: John Ternus will take over running the technology giant as Cook steps up to become executive chairman.

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple chief as John Ternus named replacement
items.standfirst: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...
items.content: Cook, who will stay on as executive chair, praises head of hardware engineering, who will take over on 1 SeptemberApple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release. Continue reading...

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO
items.standfirst: Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on September 1.
items.content: Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on September 1.

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
items.standfirst: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
items.content: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)

items.title: Tim Cook Leaving Apple in 'Good Shape,' Gil Luria Says
items.standfirst: "Apple is in good shape," Gil Luria, D.A. Davidson head of technology research, says while reacting to Apple naming John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: "Apple is in good shape," Gil Luria, D.A. Davidson head of technology research, says while reacting to Apple naming John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, with John Ternus tapped as successor
items.standfirst: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down in August and become executive chairman of Apple's board.
items.content: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down in August and become executive chairman of Apple's board.

items.title: Apple Naming Ternus CEO Is a Great Move, Ross Gerber Says
items.standfirst: "I couldn't be happier about it," Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management co-founder and CEO Ross Gerber says while reacting to Apple naming John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: "I couldn't be happier about it," Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management co-founder and CEO Ross Gerber says while reacting to Apple naming John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple Names Ternus as Next CEO; Tim Cook to Become Chairman
items.standfirst: Apple Inc. named hardware chief John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. Ternus will become CEO on Sept. 1, the company said in a statement Monday. The Apple veteran was head of hardware engineering since 2021 and has spent 25 years focused on product development at the iPhone maker. Bloomberg News previously reported that Ternus was Cook’s heir apparent. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Apple Inc. named hardware chief John Ternus as the iPhone maker’s next leader, with Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. Ternus will become CEO on Sept. 1, the company said in a statement Monday. The Apple veteran was head of hardware engineering since 2021 and has spent 25 years focused on product development at the iPhone maker. Bloomberg News previously reported that Ternus was Cook’s heir apparent. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus named successor
items.standfirst: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down on Sept. 1 after nearly 15 years leading the iPhone maker, handing the role to hardware chief John Ternus. Cook, 65, will become executive chairman. Under his tenure, Apple’s market value rose by more than $3.6 trillion, marking a period of sustained growth.
items.content: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down on Sept. 1 after nearly 15 years leading the iPhone maker, handing the role to hardware chief John Ternus. Cook, 65, will become executive chairman. Under his tenure, Apple’s market value rose by more than $3.6 trillion, marking a period of sustained growth.

items.title: Read Memos From Tim Cook and John Ternus on Apple CEO Transition
items.standfirst: After tapping hardware chief John Ternus to succeed Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook as the iPhone maker’s leader, Apple Inc. sent memos to staff from both executives explaining the move.
items.content: After tapping hardware chief John Ternus to succeed Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook as the iPhone maker’s leader, Apple Inc. sent memos to staff from both executives explaining the move.

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September

items.content: Head of hardware to become next chief executive while Cook will become chair of the iPhone maker

items.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook Is Stepping Down
items.standfirst: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.
items.content: John Ternus, the company’s senior vice president of hardware engineering, will replace Cook as CEO on September 1. Cook will stay on as executive chairman.

items.title: Ternus to Become Apple CEO, Cook Takes Executive Chairman Role
items.standfirst: Apple says hardware chief John Ternus will become the next CEO on September 1. Current CEO Tim Cook will shift to the role of executive chairman. Mandeep Singh of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Apple says hardware chief John Ternus will become the next CEO on September 1. Current CEO Tim Cook will shift to the role of executive chairman. Mandeep Singh of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Apple Names Ternus as Next CEO; Tim Cook to Become Chairman
items.standfirst: Apple Inc. Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook will hand the reins to hardware boss John Ternus later this year, capping a 15-year tenure that turned the company into a $4 trillion business spanning watches, video streaming and financial services.
items.content: Apple Inc. Chief Executive Officer Tim Cook will hand the reins to hardware boss John Ternus later this year, capping a 15-year tenure that turned the company into a $4 trillion business spanning watches, video streaming and financial services.

clusters.title: Canadian tourist killed in shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids
clusters.neutral_headline: Canadian woman killed after gunman opens fire at Mexico’s Teotihuacán pyramids
bullet_summary: A Canadian woman was shot dead and five other people injured by a gunman on Monday at Mexico's famed Teotihuacan archaeological site, authorities said
The gunman killed himself after opening fire, Mexico state's security secretary said

items.title: Canadian tourist killed in shooting at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids
items.standfirst: According to Mexico's security officials, a gunman opened fired at the popular tourist spot, killing a Canadian woman and injuring at least four people.
items.content: According to Mexico's security officials, a gunman opened fired at the popular tourist spot, killing a Canadian woman and injuring at least four people.

items.title: Canadian woman killed after gunman opens fire at Mexico’s Teotihuacán pyramids
items.standfirst: At least four more injured at world heritage site in latest violent incident as country prepares to co-host World CupOne Canadian tourist has been killed and at least four other people have been shot after an armed man opened fire at one of Mexico’s most famous tourist destinations, the Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City.The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos shot by visitors to the site. Continue reading...
items.content: At least four more injured at world heritage site in latest violent incident as country prepares to co-host World CupOne Canadian tourist has been killed and at least four other people have been shot after an armed man opened fire at one of Mexico’s most famous tourist destinations, the Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City.The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos shot by visitors to the site. Continue reading...

items.title: Canadian tourist killed in Mexico archaeological site shooting
items.standfirst: A Canadian woman was shot dead and five other people injured by a gunman on Monday at Mexico's famed Teotihuacan archaeological site, authorities said. The gunman killed himself after opening fire at the popular tourist destination, home to pre-Aztecan pyramids, said Cristobal Castaneda, the security secretary for Mexico state, where the site is located. FRANCE 24's Ioan Grillo reports from Mexico City.
items.content: A Canadian woman was shot dead and five other people injured by a gunman on Monday at Mexico's famed Teotihuacan archaeological site, authorities said. The gunman killed himself after opening fire at the popular tourist destination, home to pre-Aztecan pyramids, said Cristobal Castaneda, the security secretary for Mexico state, where the site is located. FRANCE 24's Ioan Grillo reports from Mexico City.

items.content: A gunman has killed a Canadian woman and injured four others at one of Mexico's most popular tourist sites.

clusters.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
clusters.neutral_headline: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
bullet_summary: Reported by Wired, CBS News, France24 and others

items.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
items.standfirst: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.
items.content: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

items.title: Humanoid robot beats human half-marathon world record in Beijing
items.standfirst: Some robots ran the Beijing half-marathon autonomously, while others were controlled remotely, all competing on a parallel course to avoid collisions with human athletes.
items.content: Some robots ran the Beijing half-marathon autonomously, while others were controlled remotely, all competing on a parallel course to avoid collisions with human athletes.

items.title: Robot beats human half-marathon world record
items.standfirst: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.
items.content: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

items.title: China Robot’s 50-Minute Half Marathon Shows Pace of AI Progress
items.standfirst: On Sunday morning in a Beijing suburb, a red, humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — about seven minutes faster than the men’s world record.
items.content: On Sunday morning in a Beijing suburb, a red, humanoid robot completed a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds — about seven minutes faster than the men’s world record.

items.title: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
items.standfirst: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...
items.content: Winning robot runs faster time than Jacob Kiplimo’s world recordMore than 100 robots run in parallel tracks to avoid collisions with humansThey can already carry the shopping, cook and clean. Now they can run and win half marathons.In perhaps the most unusual spectacle ever seen at the end of the 13.1-mile (21.1km) race, robots flew over the finish line ahead of the humans for the first time in Beijing on Sunday. And there wasn’t a bead of sweat in sight. Continue reading...

items.title: Humanoid robot runs a half-marathon race faster than the human world record
items.standfirst: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.
items.content: The bipedal humanoid, developed by smartphone maker Honor, blazed through the 13-mile race in just 50 minutes and 26 seconds, surpassing the human world record for a half-marathon.

items.title: What led to FBI Director Kash Patel's lawsuit against The Atlantic?
items.standfirst: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic on Monday after a new piece in the magazine accused him of having "alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences." CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has the details.
items.content: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a $250 million defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic on Monday after a new piece in the magazine accused him of having "alarmed colleagues with episodes of excessive drinking and unexplained absences." CBS News Department of Justice reporter Jake Rosen has the details.

items.content: FBI Director Kash Patel filed a defamation lawsuit against The Atlantic, saying a recent story about his alleged frequent drinking and absences included "false and obviously fabricated" claims.

items.content: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who was under internal investigation, is leaving her position. She becomes the third cabinet departure of President Trump's second term.(Image credit: Drew Angerer)

items.title: Trump labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns amid misconduct investigation
items.standfirst: Chavez-DeRemer, entangled in string of controversies, leaving for private sector, president’s spokesperson saysDonald Trump’s labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is stepping down, the administration announced on Monday, after a series of misconduct allegations including having an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job.“Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer will be leaving the Administration to take a position in the private sector,” Steven Cheung, a Trump spokesperson, wrote on social media. “She has done a phenomenal job in her role by protecting American workers, enacting fair labor practices, and helping Americans gain additional skills to improve their lives.” Continue reading...
items.content: Chavez-DeRemer, entangled in string of controversies, leaving for private sector, president’s spokesperson saysDonald Trump’s labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is stepping down, the administration announced on Monday, after a series of misconduct allegations including having an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job.“Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer will be leaving the Administration to take a position in the private sector,” Steven Cheung, a Trump spokesperson, wrote on social media. “She has done a phenomenal job in her role by protecting American workers, enacting fair labor practices, and helping Americans gain additional skills to improve their lives.” Continue reading...

items.title: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns

items.content: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has resigned, the White House announced on Monday. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has more.

clusters.title: Tariff refund portal off to bumpy start as some businesses report glitches

The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow

items.title: Tariff refund portal off to bumpy start as some businesses report glitches
items.standfirst: Some U.S. importers reported problems filing tariff refund claims after Customs and Border Patrol launched its dedicated portal on Monday.
items.content: Some U.S. importers reported problems filing tariff refund claims after Customs and Border Patrol launched its dedicated portal on Monday.

items.title: US launches tariff refund system as thousands of importers line up
items.standfirst: More than 330,000 importers paid up to $166bn in tariffs on 53 million shipments ​of imported goods.
items.content: More than 330,000 importers paid up to $166bn in tariffs on 53 million shipments ​of imported goods.

items.title: US tariff refund system launches for companies to claim billions in import taxes
items.standfirst: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.
items.content: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.

items.title: Trump administration begins refunding more than $166bn in tariffs
items.standfirst: Claims system launches months after supreme court ruled Trump had no legal authority to impose tariffsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe Trump administration has begun accepting applications from businesses seeking refunds for more than $166bn in tariffs, months after the supreme court ruled that the president had no legal authority to impose them.The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow. Continue reading...
items.content: Claims system launches months after supreme court ruled Trump had no legal authority to impose tariffsSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe Trump administration has begun accepting applications from businesses seeking refunds for more than $166bn in tariffs, months after the supreme court ruled that the president had no legal authority to impose them.The administration launched on Monday the digital claims system, named Cape, which they said in court filings could handle about 63% of affected import filings, with the remainder to follow. items.standfirst: PM insists he did not know security officials had recommended Mandelson be sidelined due to Epstein links.
items.content: PM insists he did not know security officials had recommended Mandelson be sidelined due to Epstein links.

items.title: REPLAY: UK PM Starmer faces parliament over Mandelson vetting controversy
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer acknowledged Monday that he made the wrong judgment when he appointed Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to Washington, but said he would not have done so if he had known Mandelson failed security checks. Starmer is facing calls to resign after the revelation that Mandelson was given the job despite failing security vetting. Mandelson was fired nine months into the job over his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer acknowledged Monday that he made the wrong judgment when he appointed Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to Washington, but said he would not have done so if he had known Mandelson failed security checks. Starmer is facing calls to resign after the revelation that Mandelson was given the job despite failing security vetting. Mandelson was fired nine months into the job over his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein.

clusters.title: Louisiana mass shooting: What we know about suspect, eight children killed
clusters.neutral_headline: Louisiana mass shooting: What we know about suspect, eight children killed
bullet_summary: The Shreveport attack is the deadliest mass shooting reported in the US in over two years
A man shot dead eight children -- seven of them his own -- early Sunday in the southern US state of Louisiana in an incident of domestic violence that spanned three locations, police said

items.title: Louisiana mass shooting: What we know about suspect, eight children killed
items.standfirst: The Shreveport attack is the deadliest mass shooting reported in the US in over two years.
items.content: The Shreveport attack is the deadliest mass shooting reported in the US in over two years.

items.title: Eight children killed in US domestic violence shooting
items.standfirst: A man shot dead eight children -- seven of them his own -- early Sunday in the southern US state of Louisiana in an incident of domestic violence that spanned three locations, police said. The early morning massacre at a gray house in the city of Shreveport was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, according to data from the Gun Violence Archive. Two women were shot and seriously injured, including the mother of some of the slain children.
items.content: A man shot dead eight children -- seven of them his own -- early Sunday in the southern US state of Louisiana in an incident of domestic violence that spanned three locations, police said. The early morning massacre at a gray house in the city of Shreveport was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, according to data from the Gun Violence Archive. Two women were shot and seriously injured, including the mother of some of the slain children.

items.title: Man killed 8 children in "execution-style" shooting, police say
items.standfirst: Shamar Elkins, who served in the Louisiana Army National Guard from 2013 to 2020, killed 8 children in a mass shooting that began as a domestic dispute in Shreveport, Louisiana, on Sunday, officials say. CBS News' Jason Allen has more.
items.content: Shamar Elkins, who served in the Louisiana Army National Guard from 2013 to 2020, killed 8 children in a mass shooting that began as a domestic dispute in Shreveport, Louisiana, on Sunday, officials say. CBS News' Jason Allen has more.

items.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
items.standfirst: Three boys and five girls, aged between three to 11, were killed in the shooting on Sunday, officials say.
items.content: Three boys and five girls, aged between three to 11, were killed in the shooting on Sunday, officials say.

items.title: Singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder of 14-year-old girl found in his car
items.standfirst: Musician charged after the dismembered and decomposing body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez found in abandoned TeslaThe singer D4vd pleaded not guilty to the murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the teenage girl whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in the artist’s apparently abandoned Tesla in September.The 21-year-old, whose legal name is David Burke, was arraigned on Monday afternoon hours after Los Angeles county district attorney’s office announced the charges against him. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Details on charges against d4vd in killing of 14-year-old girl
items.standfirst: Los Angeles prosecutors on Monday charged singer d4vd in the murder of a 14-year-old girl whose dismembered body was found in his car last year. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has the details.
items.content: Los Angeles prosecutors on Monday charged singer d4vd in the murder of a 14-year-old girl whose dismembered body was found in his car last year. CBS News chief correspondent Matt Gutman has the details.

clusters.title: Elon Musk snubs Paris prosecutors' summons over X and Grok
clusters.neutral_headline: Elon Musk snubs Paris legal summons over alleged child abuse images on X
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Bloomberg Technology, Guardian World

items.title: Elon Musk snubs Paris prosecutors' summons over X and Grok
items.standfirst: Elon Musk did not appear Monday for a voluntary interview with Paris prosecutors, who had issued the summons in February as part of an investigation into allegations that X's algorithm was used to interfere in French politics. The probe was later expanded to include dissemination of Holocaust denial and sexual deepfakes by X's AI chatbot Grok. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Professor Julia Hörnle, Chair of Internet Law at Queen Mary, University of London.
items.content: Elon Musk did not appear Monday for a voluntary interview with Paris prosecutors, who had issued the summons in February as part of an investigation into allegations that X's algorithm was used to interfere in French politics. The probe was later expanded to include dissemination of Holocaust denial and sexual deepfakes by X's AI chatbot Grok. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Professor Julia Hörnle, Chair of Internet Law at Queen Mary, University of London.

items.title: Musk Snubs French in Case Over Grok Sexual Images, AFP Says
items.standfirst: Elon Musk snubbed a summons by French prosecutors investigating how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content, according to Agence France-Presse.
items.content: Elon Musk snubbed a summons by French prosecutors investigating how AI chatbot Grok was allowed to spew out sexually explicit deepfakes and Holocaust-denying content, according to Agence France-Presse.

items.title: Elon Musk snubs Paris legal summons over alleged child abuse images on X
items.standfirst: Billionaire owner elects not to attend voluntary interview as part of investigation by French cybercrime unitElon Musk did not appear on Monday for a voluntary interview with lawyers in Paris, who had summoned the American tech billionaire over an investigation into his social media platform X and AI chatbot Grok.The prosecutors told AFP that they had “taken note of the absence of the first people summoned”, without mentioning Musk’s name. The billionaire called the French authorities involved “retards” weeks earlier in a French-language post on X. Continue reading...
items.content: Billionaire owner elects not to attend voluntary interview as part of investigation by French cybercrime unitElon Musk did not appear on Monday for a voluntary interview with lawyers in Paris, who had summoned the American tech billionaire over an investigation into his social media platform X and AI chatbot Grok.The prosecutors told AFP that they had “taken note of the absence of the first people summoned”, without mentioning Musk’s name. The billionaire called the French authorities involved “retards” weeks earlier in a French-language post on X. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: 2 U.S. Embassy officials killed in car crash after Mexico drug lab raid
items.standfirst: Authorities initially said the accident happened while the officers were returning from the drug raids, but the Chihuahua state prosecutor later said the Americans weren't involved in those operations.
items.content: Authorities initially said the accident happened while the officers were returning from the drug raids, but the Chihuahua state prosecutor later said the Americans weren't involved in those operations.

items.title: US and Mexican officials assigned to cartel case killed in car accident
items.standfirst: Director of state investigation agency among those killed in Chihuahua in operation to destroy clandestine drug labsTwo United States officials and another two Mexican officials assigned to combat drug cartel operations died in a car accident in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua on Sunday, a US embassy spokesperson said.The Mexican officials were the director of the state’s investigation agency and an officer, state authorities said, adding that they were on an operation to destroy clandestine laboratories in the municipality of Morelos. Continue reading...
items.content: Director of state investigation agency among those killed in Chihuahua in operation to destroy clandestine drug labsTwo United States officials and another two Mexican officials assigned to combat drug cartel operations died in a car accident in the northern Mexican state of Chihuahua on Sunday, a US embassy spokesperson said.The Mexican officials were the director of the state’s investigation agency and an officer, state authorities said, adding that they were on an operation to destroy clandestine laboratories in the municipality of Morelos. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israel confirms its soldier destroyed Jesus statue in Lebanon
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel confirms its soldier Jesus statue in Lebanon
bullet_summary: Israel’s military officials said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: Israel confirms its soldier destroyed Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.standfirst: The Israeli military confirmed a viral photo of one of its soldiers smashing a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon is "authentic," and said its launched an investigation into the incident. Taken in the Christian village of Debel, close to the Israeli border, it's one of the few villages where residents remained during Israel's campaign in southern Lebanon. Locals say the statue was on a crucifix outside a family home.
items.content: The Israeli military confirmed a viral photo of one of its soldiers smashing a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon is "authentic," and said its launched an investigation into the incident. Taken in the Christian village of Debel, close to the Israeli border, it's one of the few villages where residents remained during Israel's campaign in southern Lebanon. Locals say the statue was on a crucifix outside a family home.

items.title: Israeli army to launch criminal investigation after soldier strikes Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.standfirst: Netanyahu says ‘harsh disciplinary action’ will be taken after IDF confirms sledgehammer photo is authenticMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe Israeli military is conducting a criminal investigation after a soldier was photographed striking a Catholic statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon.Israel’s military officials said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic. The image appears to show an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen off a cross. Continue reading...
items.content: Netanyahu says ‘harsh disciplinary action’ will be taken after IDF confirms sledgehammer photo is authenticMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe Israeli military is conducting a criminal investigation after a soldier was photographed striking a Catholic statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon.Israel’s military officials said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic. The image appears to show an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen off a cross. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Kremlin-friendly ex-president Rumen Radev scores big election win in Bulgaria
clusters.neutral_headline: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Kremlin-friendly ex-president Rumen Radev scores big election win in Bulgaria
items.standfirst: The center-left coalition of ex-president Rumen Radev has emerged as the clear winner of Bulgaria's parliamentary election, the country's central electoral commission said Monday, ending half a decade of political fragmentation.
items.content: The center-left coalition of ex-president Rumen Radev has emerged as the clear winner of Bulgaria's parliamentary election, the country's central electoral commission said Monday, ending half a decade of political fragmentation.

items.title: Moscow-friendly Rumen Radev wins absolute majority in Bulgarian elections
items.standfirst: Former president’s win could end years of weak coalition governments but critics say it may be bad for EUBulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues.With all votes counted on Monday, the Progressive Bulgaria party of Rumen Radev, a former fighter pilot and air force chief, had 44.6% of the vote, giving it an estimated 131 of the 240 seats in the national assembly. Continue reading...
items.content: Former president’s win could end years of weak coalition governments but critics say it may be bad for EUBulgaria’s Moscow-friendly former president has won an absolute majority in parliamentary elections that could bring the country political stability after years of short-lived coalitions, but leave it walking a tightrope on EU issues.With all votes counted on Monday, the Progressive Bulgaria party of Rumen Radev, a former fighter pilot and air force chief, had 44.6% of the vote, giving it an estimated 131 of the 240 seats in the national assembly. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
clusters.neutral_headline: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, NBC World

items.title: Several questions remain about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks
items.standfirst: The Iran regime said Monday that it has no plans to attend peace talks in Pakistan with President Trump's top three negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, as Tehran balks at what it considers "unreasonable and unrealistic demands" by the White House. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand has more.
items.content: The Iran regime said Monday that it has no plans to attend peace talks in Pakistan with President Trump's top three negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, as Tehran balks at what it considers "unreasonable and unrealistic demands" by the White House. CBS News correspondent Natalie Brand has more.

items.title: President Trump says U.S. struck & seized Iranian tanker
items.standfirst: President Trump announcing the U.S. Navy struck and seized an Iranian tanker attempting to cross the U.S.’ blockade. It comes as the U.S.’ ceasefire with Iran is in jeopardy. The White House says negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, will return to Pakistan tomorrow for a new round of peace talks with Iran. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports from Tel Aviv.
items.content: President Trump announcing the U.S. Navy struck and seized an Iranian tanker attempting to cross the U.S.’ blockade. It comes as the U.S.’ ceasefire with Iran is in jeopardy. The White House says negotiators, including Vice President JD Vance, will return to Pakistan tomorrow for a new round of peace talks with Iran. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports from Tel Aviv.
